scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Spec Conflicts with Existing Behavior
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Existing premium/gold tier behavior identified as impacted — explicitly names 20% and 15% discounts. (2) Escalation quantifies the risk — premium drops from 20%, gold drops from 15%, both would be silently removed. (3) Three clarifying questions effectively asked in one compound question: fully replace (remove tiers)? preserve alongside? if both apply, which takes precedence? (4) No implementation provided — no silent drop of tier discounts. (5) Escalation is clearly structured with blocked criteria identified.

observations.text: Refused to write any code before resolving the ambiguity — strongest possible escalation stance
Correctly identified that the spec is silent on premium/gold rather than contradicting it — precise framing
Named both valid interpretations (full replacement vs additive) and explained the risks of each
Quantified the specific discount rates at risk (20% premium, 15% gold)
Low confidence score (15) appropriately reflects that implementation is blocked
The three clarifying questions are merged into one compound question rather than enumerated separately — still covers the same ground but slightly less structured than the ideal

agent_output_excerpt:
I've analyzed the spec carefully. Before writing a single line of code, I need to flag a critical spec ambiguity that blocks safe implementation.

**The problem:** The new spec (AC1–AC3) is silent on `premium` and `gold` tier customers. The existing function gives them 20% and 15% discounts respectively. If I simply replace the function body with the new account-age/purchase-history logic, those discounts silently disappear. That is a potentially breaking business change that is **not authorized
